title: clematis villosa subsp. stanleyi
intro: Clematis villosa subsp. stanleyi, also known as the hairy clematis or Virginian clematis, is a flowering plant native to North America. This plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of clematis villosa subsp. stanleyi include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of clematis villosa subsp. stanleyi include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves or flowers in hot water, and tinctures, which can be created by soaking the plant material in a solvent such as ethanol.
